title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Speed Booster Test

questions.question: The base of a right pyramid is an equilateral triangle of side 10‚ąö3 cm. If the total surface area of the pyramid is 270‚ąö3 sq. cm, its height is ?
questions.explanation: Given: The base of the pyramid is an equilateral triangle with side 10310\sqrt{3}103‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč cm. The total surface area of the pyramid is 2703 270\sqrt{3}2703‚Äč‚Äč sq. cm. We need to find the height of the pyramid. Concept Used: The total surface area of a pyramid is the sum of the base area and the lateral surface area. For a pyramid with an equilateral triangle base: Base area of an equilateral triangle is: ‚ÄčAbase=34√óside2A_{\text{base}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2Abase‚Äč=43‚Äč‚Äč√óside2‚Äč‚Äč Total surface area of a pyramid: ‚ÄčAtotal=Abase+AlateralA_{\text{total}} = A_{\text{base}} + A_{\text{lateral}}Atotal‚Äč=Abase‚Äč+Alateral‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč The lateral surface area depends on the slant height and the perimeter of the base. Area of an equilateral triangle: ‚ÄčAbase=34√óside2A_{\text{base}} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2Abase‚Äč=43‚Äč‚Äč√óside2‚Äč‚Äč ‚ÄčAtotal=Abase+AlateralA_{\text{total}} = A_{\text{base}} + A_{\text{lateral}}Atotal‚Äč=Abase‚Äč+Alateral‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Pythagoras theorem for the height of the pyramid: ‚Äčh2+(inradius)2=slant height2h^2 + \left( inradius \right)^2 = \text{slant height}^2h2+(inradius)2=slant height2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Area of Base = 34√ó(103)2\frac{\sqrt3}4 √ó (10\sqrt3) ^ 243‚Äč‚Äč√ó(103‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč = 75375\sqrt3753‚Äč‚Äč cm2 Lateral surface area = 2703‚ąí753 270\sqrt3 - 75\sqrt32703‚Äč‚ąí753‚Äč‚Äč = 1953 195\sqrt31953‚Äč‚Äč cm2 Area of each lateral surface = 19533\frac{195\sqrt3}331953‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 65365\sqrt3 653‚Äč cm2 => 12√ó103√óh=653\frac12 √ó 10\sqrt3 √ó h = 65\sqrt321‚Äč√ó103‚Äč√óh=653‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč => h = 13 cm (slant height) Inradius = side of triangle23=10323=5\frac{side\ of\ triangle}{2\sqrt3} = \frac{10\sqrt3}{2\sqrt3} = 523‚Äčside of triangle‚Äč=23‚Äč103‚Äč‚Äč=5 Now, height of pyramid = 132‚ąí52\sqrt{13 ^ 2 - 5 ^ 2}132‚ąí52‚Äč‚Äč = 12 cm ‚Äč

questions.question: A can finish a piece of work in 36 days and B in 72 days. They begin the work together, but A leaves 6 days before the completion of the work. In how many total days the complete work will be done?
questions.explanation: Given: Time taken by A = 36 days Time taken by B = 72 days A leaves 6 days before the completion of the work. Formula Used: Work Done = Rate of Work √ó Time Solution: Let the total number of days taken to complete the work be x. Since A leaves 6 days before completion, A works for (x - 6) days. B works for the entire duration of x days. Formulate the work equation: ‚Äčx‚ąí636+x72=1\frac{x - 6}{36} + \frac{x}{72} = 136x‚ąí6‚Äč+72x‚Äč=1‚Äč‚Äč 2(x - 6) + x = 72 2x - 12 + x = 72 3x = 84 x = 28 The complete work will be done in 28 days. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Method: ‚Äč ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a7f8739a-beb7-499a-af3b-a427b15f4688.png)

questions.question: A certain sum amounts to ‚āĻ4,620 after two years at a certain rate of simple interest and to ‚āĻ4,725 after two and a half years at the same rate. Find the annual percentage rate.
questions.explanation: Given: Amount after 2 years A1 A_1 A1‚Äč‚Äč= ‚āĻ4,620 Amount after 2.5 years A2A_2 A2‚Äč‚Äč= ‚āĻ4,725 Time difference = 0.5 years Formula Used: Simple Interest formula: ‚ÄčA=P+SI=P+P√ór√ót100A = P + \text{SI} = P + \frac{P \times r \times t}{100}A=P+SI=P+100P√ór√ót‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč where P = principal, r = annual rate, t = time in years. Solution: Let P be the principal and r be the annual rate. For 2 years: ‚Äč4620=P+P√ór√ó21004620 = P + \frac{P \times r \times 2}{100}4620=P+100P√ór√ó2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč For 2.5 years: ‚Äč4725=P+P√ór√ó2.51004725 = P + \frac{P \times r \times 2.5}{100}4725=P+100P√ór√ó2.5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Now, ‚Äč4725‚ąí4620=P√ór√ó2.5100‚ąíP√ór√ó2100 105=P√ór√ó0.5100 P√ór=105√ó1000.5=210004725 - 4620 = \frac{P \times r \times 2.5}{100} - \frac{P \times r \times 2}{100}\\ \ \\105 = \frac{P \times r \times 0.5}{100}\\ \ \\P \times r = \frac{105 \times 100}{0.5} = 210004725‚ąí4620=100P√ór√ó2.5‚Äč‚ąí100P√ór√ó2‚Äč 105=100P√ór√ó0.5‚Äč P√ór=0.5105√ó100‚Äč=21000‚Äč‚Äč Substituting ( P√ór=21000P \times r = 21000P√ór=21000‚Äč ) into the first equation: ‚Äč4620=P+21000√ó2100 4620=P+420 P=4620‚ąí420=42004620 = P + \frac{21000 \times 2}{100}\\\ \\4620 = P + 420\\ \ \\P = 4620 - 420 = 42004620=P+10021000√ó2‚Äč 4620=P+420 P=4620‚ąí420=4200‚Äč‚Äč Now, substituting P = 4200 into P√ór=21000 P \times r = 21000 P√ór=21000‚Äč: ‚Äč4200√ór=21000 r=210004200=54200 \times r = 21000\\ \ \\r = \frac{21000}{4200} = 54200√ór=21000 r=420021000‚Äč=5‚Äč‚Äč Thus, The annual percentage rate is 5%. Alternate Method: 4725 - 4620 = 105 ( S I of 0.5 year) So for 2 year S I will be = 105 √ó2√ó2=420\times 2 \times 2 = 420√ó2√ó2=420 Principal = 4620 - 420 = 4200 From the SI formula Rate = 420√ó1004200√ó2=5%\frac{420 \times 100}{4200 \times 2} = 5\%4200√ó2420√ó100‚Äč=5%‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: A table whose marked price ‚āĻ300 is sold at two successive discounts of 20% and 10%. If in addition, a discount of 5% is offered on cash payment, then the cash price for the table is:
questions.explanation: Given: Marked price of the table = Rs. 300 Successive discounts: 20% and 10% Additional discount on cash payment: 5% Formula Used: ‚ÄčSelling Price (SP)=Marked Price (MP)√ó(1‚ąíFirst Discount%100)√ó(1‚ąíSecond Discount%100)√ó(1‚ąíCash Discount%100)\text{Selling Price (SP)} = \text{Marked Price (MP)} \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{First Discount\%}}{100} \right) \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{Second Discount\%}}{100} \right) \times \left( 1 - \frac{\text{Cash Discount\%}}{100} \right)Selling Price (SP)=Marked Price (MP)√ó(1‚ąí100First Discount%‚Äč)√ó(1‚ąí100Second Discount%‚Äč)√ó(1‚ąí100Cash Discount%‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč Solution: According to the question: Cash price for the table = 300√ó80100√ó90100√ó95100=205.20300 \times \frac{80}{100} \times \frac{90}{100} \times \frac{95}{100} = 205.20300√ó10080‚Äč√ó10090‚Äč√ó10095‚Äč=205.20‚Äč‚Äč ![](https://ts-cdn.adda247.com/1699350725597image70.png)

questions.question: Find the relation between x and y such that the point (x, y) is equidistant from (6, 2) and (4, 6).
questions.explanation: Given: Point A = (6, 2) Point B = (4, 6) Point (x, y) is equidistant from both A and B Concept Used: A point is equidistant from two given points if its distance from each of those two points is the same. Formula Used: Distance between two point = (x2‚ąíx1)2+(y2‚ąíy1)2\sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}(x2‚Äč‚ąíx1‚Äč)2+(y2‚Äč‚ąíy1‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Distance from (x, y) to (6, 2) = (x‚ąí6)2+(y‚ąí2)2 \sqrt{(x - 6)^2 + (y - 2)^2} \\(x‚ąí6)2+(y‚ąí2)2‚Äč‚Äč Distance from (x, y) to (4, 6) =(x‚ąí4)2+(y‚ąí6)2= \sqrt{(x - 4)^2 + (y - 6)^2} \\=(x‚ąí4)2+(y‚ąí6)2‚Äč‚Äč As distance are equal from both points: ‚Äč(x‚ąí6)2+(y‚ąí2)2=(x‚ąí4)2+(y‚ąí6)2x2‚ąí12x+36+y2‚ąí4y+4=x2‚ąí8x+16+y2‚ąí12y+36‚ąí12x‚ąí4y+40=‚ąí8x‚ąí12y+52‚ąí4x+8y‚ąí12=0‚ąíx+2y‚ąí3=0=&gtx‚ąí2y=3(x - 6)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = (x - 4)^2 + (y - 6)^2 \\x^2 - 12x + 36 + y^2 - 4y + 4 = x^2 - 8x + 16 + y^2 - 12y + 36 \\-12x - 4y + 40 = -8x - 12y + 52 \\-4x + 8y - 12 = 0 \\-x + 2y - 3 = 0 \\\Rightarrow x - 2y = 3(x‚ąí6)2+(y‚ąí2)2=(x‚ąí4)2+(y‚ąí6)2x2‚ąí12x+36+y2‚ąí4y+4=x2‚ąí8x+16+y2‚ąí12y+36‚ąí12x‚ąí4y+40=‚ąí8x‚ąí12y+52‚ąí4x+8y‚ąí12=0‚ąíx+2y‚ąí3=0=&gtx‚ąí2y=3‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: An airplane is in level flight at an altitude of 1.5 km from the ground is observed at an angle of elevation of 60¬į. After 10 seconds, its angle of elevation is observed to be 30¬į. The speed of the airplane in km/hr is:
questions.explanation: Given: Altitude of the airplane, h = 1.5 km = 1500 m Initial angle of elevation, őł1=60‚ąė\theta_1 = 60^{\circ}őł1‚Äč=60‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Final angle of elevation after 10 seconds, őł2=30‚ąė\theta_2 = 30^{\circ}őł2‚Äč=30‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Time taken, t = 10 s Formula Used: ‚Äčtan‚Ā°őł=OppositeAdwacent\tan \theta = \frac{\text{Opposite}}{\text{Adwacent}}tanőł=AdwacentOpposite‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Speed = DistanceTime \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Time}}TimeDistance‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/91ef4a36-3db9-4e07-b92f-39e50b91c858.png) Let the initial horizontal distance be x and the distance traveled by the plane in 10 seconds be d. From the first position: ‚Äčtan‚Ā°60‚ąė=1500x\tan 60^{\circ} = \frac{1500}{x}tan60‚ąė=x1500‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč3=1500x\sqrt{3} = \frac{1500}{x}3‚Äč=x1500‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč x = 15003=5003 \frac{1500}{\sqrt{3}} = 500\sqrt{3}3‚Äč1500‚Äč=5003‚Äč‚Äč m From the second position: ‚Äčtan‚Ā°30‚ąė=1500x+d\tan 30^{\circ} = \frac{1500}{x + d}tan30‚ąė=x+d1500‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč13=1500x+d\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{1500}{x + d}3‚Äč1‚Äč=x+d1500‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč x+d=15003x + d = 1500\sqrt{3}x+d=15003‚Äč‚Äč m Now, find the distance traveled d: d = 15003‚ąí50031500\sqrt{3} - 500\sqrt{3}15003‚Äč‚ąí5003‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč d = 100031000\sqrt{3}10003‚Äč‚Äč m Calculate the speed in m/s: Speed = 1000310=1003\frac{1000\sqrt{3}}{10} = 100\sqrt{3}1010003‚Äč‚Äč=1003‚Äč‚Äč m/s Convert the speed to km/hr: Speed = 1003√ó185 100\sqrt{3} \times \frac{18}{5}1003‚Äč√ó518‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 203√ó18=360320\sqrt{3} \times 18 = 360\sqrt{3}203‚Äč√ó18=3603‚Äč‚Äč km/hr Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: When x is subtracted from each of 55, 50, 23 and 22, the resulting numbers, in this order, are in proportion. What is the fourth proportion of 3, 7 and x?
questions.explanation: Given: (55 ‚ąí x), (50 ‚ąí x), (23 ‚ąí x), (22 ‚ąí x) are in proportion. Formula Used: If a, b, c and d are in proportion then ab=cd\frac{a}{b} = \frac{c}{d}ba‚Äč=dc‚Äč Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by=b√óca\text{Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by} = \frac{b \times c}{a}Fourth proportional of a, b and c is given by=ab√óc‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚Äč55‚ąíx50‚ąíx=23‚ąíx22‚ąíx (55‚ąíx)(22‚ąíx)=(50‚ąíx)(23‚ąíx) 1210‚ąí77x=1150‚ąí73x 60=4x x=15 \frac{55 - x}{50 - x} = \frac{23 - x}{22 - x}\\\ \\(55 - x)(22 - x) = (50 - x)(23 - x)\\\ \\1210 - 77x = 1150 - 73x\\\ \\60 = 4x\\\ \\x = 15\\\ \\50‚ąíx55‚ąíx‚Äč=22‚ąíx23‚ąíx‚Äč (55‚ąíx)(22‚ąíx)=(50‚ąíx)(23‚ąíx) 1210‚ąí77x=1150‚ąí73x 60=4x x=15 ‚Äč‚Äč Fourth proportional of 3, 7, 15 = (7 √ó 15) √∑ 3 = 35

questions.question: A person sells an old laptop for Rs. 18,000, making a profit of 20% after spending Rs. 2,000 on repairs. What was the initial price at which he bought the laptop?
questions.explanation: Given: Selling Price (SP) = 18000 Profit = 20% Repair cost = 2000 Formula Used: Cost Price (CP) = SP1+Profit%100 \frac{\text{SP}}{1 + \frac{\text{Profit}\%}{100}}1+100Profit%‚ÄčSP‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Initial Price = Total CP - Repair cost Solution: Calculate the total Cost Price: CP = 180001+0.20=180001.20\frac{18000}{1 + 0.20} = \frac{18000}{1.20}1+0.2018000‚Äč=1.2018000‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč CP = 15000 The total CP includes the initial purchase price plus repair costs. Initial Price + 2000 = 15000 Initial Price = 15000 - 2000 = 13000 So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Due to decrease in manpower, the production in a factory decreases by 28%. By approximately what percentage should the working hours be increased to restore the original level of production?
questions.explanation: Given: Decrease in production (which corresponds to manpower decrease) = 28% Formula Used: Production = Manpower√óWorkingHoursManpower \times Working HoursManpower√óWorkingHours‚Äč‚Äč Required Percentage Increase = Decrease100‚ąíDecrease√ó100 \frac{Decrease}{100 - Decrease} \times 100100‚ąíDecreaseDecrease‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the initial manpower and working hours be 100 each. Original production = 10000. New manpower = 100 - 28 = 72. To restore original production, New Manpower \times New Working Hours = 10000. 72 \times New Working Hours = 10000 New Working Hours = 1000072\frac{10000}{72} 7210000‚Äč‚Äč = 138.88 Increase in working hours = 138.88...‚ąí100=38.88...%‚Čą38.89% 138.88... - 100 = 38.88...\% \approx 38.89\%138.88...‚ąí100=38.88...%‚Čą38.89%‚Äč‚Äč So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: The marks obtained by 11 students in a mathematics test are as follows: 45, 62, 58, 71, 66, 54, 60, 49, 68, 57, 63. What is the median marks?
questions.explanation: Given Marks of 11 students: 45, 62, 58, 71, 66, 54, 60, 49, 68, 57, 63 Total number of terms (n) = 11 Formula Used Median = Value of the n+12th\frac{n+1}{2}^{ th}2n+1‚Äčth‚Äč term in a sorted dataset (for odd n) Solution First, arrange the marks in ascending order: 45, 49, 54, 57, 58, 60, 62, 63, 66, 68, 71 Since n = 11 (an odd number), the median is the 11+12th\frac{11 + 1}{2} ^{th}211+1‚Äčth‚Äč term. Median = 6th term. Counting to the 6th position in the sorted list, we find the value is 60. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If a = 7 + 4‚ąö3, then evaluate a3+1a3a^3 + \frac{1}{a^3}a3+a31‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGiven: a=7+43Formula Used: a3+1a3=(a+1a)3‚ąí3(a+1a) Solution: 1a=7‚ąí43(since (7+43)(7‚ąí43)=1) a+1a=(7+43)+(7‚ąí43)=14 a3+1a3=143‚ąí3√ó14 =2744‚ąí42 =2702 \textbf{Given: } \\a = 7 + 4\sqrt{3}\\\textbf{Formula Used: }\\a^3 + \frac{1}{a^3} = \left(a + \frac{1}{a}\right)^3 - 3\left(a + \frac{1}{a}\right) \\\ \\\textbf{Solution: }\\ \frac{1}{a} = 7 - 4\sqrt{3} \quad (\text{since } (7+4\sqrt{3})(7-4\sqrt{3}) = 1) \\\ \\a + \frac{1}{a} = (7 + 4\sqrt{3}) + (7 - 4\sqrt{3}) = 14\\\ \\a^3 + \frac{1}{a^3} = 14^3 - 3 \times 14\\\ \\= 2744 - 42\\\ \\= 2702\\\ \\Given: a=7+43‚ÄčFormula Used: a3+a31‚Äč=(a+a1‚Äč)3‚ąí3(a+a1‚Äč) Solution: a1‚Äč=7‚ąí43‚Äč(since (7+43‚Äč)(7‚ąí43‚Äč)=1) a+a1‚Äč=(7+43‚Äč)+(7‚ąí43‚Äč)=14 a3+a31‚Äč=143‚ąí3√ó14 =2744‚ąí42 =2702 ‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: Kiran starts business with ‚āĻ55,000 and after 7 months, Zarina woins with Kiran as her partner. After a year, the profit is divided in the ratio 8 : 5. What is Zarina's contribution in the capital?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGivenKiran‚Äôs capital=55000Kiran‚Äôs time=12Zarina‚Äôs time=12‚ąí7=5Profit ratio=8:5Formula UsedProfit Ratio=Capital√óTimeSolutionKiran‚Äôs capital-time=55000√ó12=660000Let Zarina‚Äôs capital=xZarina‚Äôs capital-time=5x6600005x=85660000√ó5=8√ó5x3300000=40xx=82500Final Answer82500\textbf{Given} \\\text{Kiran's capital} = 55000 \\\text{Kiran's time} = 12 \\\text{Zarina's time} = 12 - 7 = 5 \\\text{Profit ratio} = 8 : 5 \\[6pt]\textbf{Formula Used} \\\text{Profit Ratio} = \text{Capital} \times \text{Time} \\[6pt]\textbf{Solution} \\\text{Kiran's capital-time} = 55000 \times 12 = 660000 \\\text{Let Zarina's capital} = x \\\text{Zarina's capital-time} = 5x \\[6pt]\frac{660000}{5x} = \frac{8}{5} \\660000 \times 5 = 8 \times 5x \\3300000 = 40x \\x = 82500 \\[6pt]\textbf{Final Answer} \\\boxed{82500}GivenKiran‚Äôs capital=55000Kiran‚Äôs time=12Zarina‚Äôs time=12‚ąí7=5Profit ratio=8:5Formula UsedProfit Ratio=Capital√óTimeSolutionKiran‚Äôs capital-time=55000√ó12=660000Let Zarina‚Äôs capital=xZarina‚Äôs capital-time=5x5x660000‚Äč=58‚Äč660000√ó5=8√ó5x3300000=40xx=82500Final Answer82500‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: A rectangular sheet of a paper, 15 cm long and 10 cm wide, has squares of side 2 cm cut from each of its corners. The sheet is then folded to form a tray of depth 2 cm. Find the volume of this tray.
questions.explanation: Given: Length of sheet = 15 cm Width of sheet = 10 cm Side of square cut from corners = 2 cm Formula Used: Length of tray = Original length - 2 √ó (cut side) Width of tray = Original width - 2 √ó (cut side) Volume of cuboid = Length √ó Width √ó Height Solution: Length of the tray = 15 - 2 √ó 2 = 15 - 4 = 11 cm. Width of the tray = 10 - 2 √ó 2 = 10 - 4 = 6 cm. Height (depth) of the tray = 2 cm. Volume of the tray = 11 √ó 6 √ó 2 Volume = 66 √ó 2 = 132 cm3 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: The product of two consecutive natural numbers is 306. The greater of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: Given Product of two consecutive natural numbers = 306 Formula Used Let the consecutive natural numbers be x and x + 1 Product = x(x + 1) Solution Set up the quadratic equation based on the given information: x(x + 1) = 306 x2x^2 x2‚Äč+ x - 306 = 0 x2x^2 x2‚Äč+ 18x - 17x - 306 = 0 x(x + 18) - 17(x + 18) = 0 (x - 17)(x + 18) = 0 So, x = 17 or x = -18 Since the numbers are natural numbers, x cannot be negative. Therefore, x = 17 The two numbers are 17 and 18 The greater number is 18 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Simplify: cos‚Ā°őł(1+sin‚Ā°őł)+(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł.\frac{cos‚Ā°őł}{(1+sin‚Ā°őł )} + \frac{(1+sin‚Ā°őł)}{cos‚Ā°őł }.(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł‚Äč+cos‚Ā°őł(1+sin‚Ā°őł)‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given Expression :cos‚Ā°őł1+sin‚Ā°őł+1+sin‚Ā°őłcos‚Ā°őł: \frac{\cosőł}{1 + \sinőł} + \frac{1 + \sinőł}{\cosőł}:1+sinőłcosőł‚Äč+cosőł1+sinőł‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used ‚Äčsin‚Ā°2őł+cos‚Ā°2őł=1sec‚Ā°őł=1cos‚Ā°őł\sin^2őł + \cos^2őł = 1\\\secőł = \frac{1}{\cosőł}sin2őł+cos2őł=1secőł=cosőł1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Take the lowest common multiple (LCM) of the denominators: Expression = cos‚Ā°2őł+(1+sin‚Ā°őł)2(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł \frac{\cos^2őł + (1 + \sinőł)^2}{(1 + \sinőł)\cosőł}(1+sinőł)cosőłcos2őł+(1+sinőł)2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Expand the numerator: ‚Äč=cos‚Ā°2őł+1+sin‚Ā°2őł+2sin‚Ā°őł(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł= \frac{\cos^2őł + 1 + \sin^2őł + 2\sinőł}{(1 + \sinőł)\cosőł}=(1+sinőł)cosőłcos2őł+1+sin2őł+2sinőł‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Use the identity sin‚Ā°2őł+cos‚Ā°2őł=1:\sin^2őł + \cos^2őł = 1:sin2őł+cos2őł=1:‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=1+1+2sin‚Ā°őł(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł =2+2sin‚Ā°őł(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł =2(1+sin‚Ā°őł)(1+sin‚Ā°őł)cos‚Ā°őł =2cos‚Ā°őł=2sec‚Ā°őł= \frac{1 + 1 + 2\sinőł}{(1 + \sinőł)\cosőł}\\\ \\= \frac{2 + 2\sinőł}{(1 + \sinőł)\cosőł}\\\ \\= \frac{2(1 + \sinőł)}{(1 + \sinőł)\cosőł}\\\ \\= \frac{2}{\cosőł} = 2\secőł=(1+sinőł)cosőł1+1+2sinőł‚Äč =(1+sinőł)cosőł2+2sinőł‚Äč =(1+sinőł)cosőł2(1+sinőł)‚Äč =cosőł2‚Äč=2secőł‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: One side of a right-angled triangle is twice the other and the hypotenuse is 15 cm. Find the area (in cm¬≤) of the triangle.
questions.explanation: Given: One side is twice the other. Hypotenuse = 15 cm. Formula Used: Pythagoras theorem: Base2 + Perpendicular2 = Hypotenuse2 Area of a right-angled triangle = 12√óBase√óPerpendicular \frac{1}{2} \times \text{Base} \times \text{Perpendicular}21‚Äč√óBase√óPerpendicular‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let one side be x and the other side be 2x. Using Pythagoras theorem: ‚Äčx2+(2x)2=152 x2+4x2=225 5x2=225 x2=45x^2 + (2x)^2 = 15^2 \\ \ \\x^2 + 4x^2 = 225 \\ \ \ \\5x^2 = 225 \\ \ \\x^2 = 45x2+(2x)2=152 x2+4x2=225 5x2=225 x2=45‚Äč‚Äč Area of the right-angled triangle = 12√óx√ó2x \frac{1}{2} \times x \times 2x21‚Äč√óx√ó2x‚Äč‚Äč Area = x2x^2x2‚Äč‚Äč Substitute the value of x2x^2x2‚Äč: Area = 45 cm¬≤. Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Sooraw is three times as old as his son Arwun, After 8 years, he would be two and a half times of Arwun's age. After further 8 years, he would be times Arwun's age.
questions.explanation: Given: Sooraw (S) = 3 √ó Arwun (A) After 8 years: S + 8 = 2.5 √ó (A + 8) After 16 years: S + 16 = k √ó (A + 16) Find the value of k Solution: From the condition; S = 3A .....(1) S + 8 = 2.5(A + 8) .......(2) S + 16 = k(A + 16) ......(3) From (1): S = 3A Substitute into (2): 3A + 8 = 2.5(A + 8) => 3A + 8 = 2.5A + 20 => 0.5A = 12 => A = 24 Then, S = 3 √ó 24 = 72 After 16 years: S + 16 = 72 + 16 = 88 A + 16 = 24 + 16 = 40 Now from (3): k = (S+16)(A+16)=8840=215 \frac{(S + 16)} { (A + 16) }= \frac{88} { 40} = 2\frac15(A+16)(S+16)‚Äč=4088‚Äč=251‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: The number of diagonals of an n-sided regular polygon is 665. What is the value of ‚Äėn‚Äô?
questions.explanation: Given Number of diagonals = 665 Formula Used Number of diagonals of an n-sided regular polygon = n(n‚ąí3)2 \frac{n(n - 3)}{2}2n(n‚ąí3)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution ‚Äčn(n‚ąí3)2=665n(n‚ąí3)=1330n2‚ąí3n‚ąí1330=0\frac{n(n - 3)}{2} = 665 \\n(n - 3) = 1330\\n^2 - 3n - 1330 = 0\\2n(n‚ąí3)‚Äč=665n(n‚ąí3)=1330n2‚ąí3n‚ąí1330=0‚Äč‚Äč Factorizing the quadratic equation: ‚Äčn2‚ąí38n+35n‚ąí1330=0n(n‚ąí38)+35(n‚ąí38)=0(n‚ąí38)(n+35)=0n^2 - 38n + 35n - 1330 = 0\\n(n - 38) + 35(n - 38) = 0\\(n - 38)(n + 35) = 0\\n2‚ąí38n+35n‚ąí1330=0n(n‚ąí38)+35(n‚ąí38)=0(n‚ąí38)(n+35)=0‚Äč‚Äč Since the number of sides cannot be negative, n = 38. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A bag contains 3 red, 5 blue, and 2 green balls. Two balls are drawn one after another without replacement. Find the probability that both balls are blue.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGiven: Red Balls=3,Blue Balls=5,Green Balls=2 Total Balls=3+5+2=10 Event: Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue. Solution: Probability of 1st ball being Blue: P(B1)=510=12 After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4. Probability of 2nd ball being Blue: P(B2‚ą£B1)=49 Combined Probability: P(Both Blue)=P(B1)√óP(B2‚ą£B1)=12√ó49 =418=29 Final Answer 29\textbf{Given:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Red Balls} = 3, \quad \text{Blue Balls} = 5, \quad \text{Green Balls} = 2 \\ \space \, \\ \text{Total Balls} = 3 + 5 + 2 = 10 \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Event:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue.} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Solution:} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Probability of 1st ball being Blue:} \\ \space \, \\ P(B_1) = \frac{5}{10} = \frac{1}{2} \\ \space \, \\ \text{After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4.} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Probability of 2nd ball being Blue:} \\ \space \, \\ P(B_2 | B_1) = \frac{4}{9} \\ \space \, \\ \text{Combined Probability:} \\ \space \, \\ P(\text{Both Blue}) = P(B_1) \times P(B_2 | B_1) = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{9} \\ \space \, \\ = \frac{4}{18} = \frac{2}{9} \\ \space \, \\ \textbf{Final Answer} \\ \space \, \\ \frac{2}{9}Given: Red Balls=3,Blue Balls=5,Green Balls=2 Total Balls=3+5+2=10 Event: Two balls drawn without replacement, both are Blue. Solution: Probability of 1st ball being Blue: P(B1‚Äč)=105‚Äč=21‚Äč After 1st Blue is drawn, remaining Total = 9, remaining Blue = 4. Probability of 2nd ball being Blue: P(B2‚Äč‚ą£B1‚Äč)=94‚Äč Combined Probability: P(Both Blue)=P(B1‚Äč)√óP(B2‚Äč‚ą£B1‚Äč)=21‚Äč√ó94‚Äč =184‚Äč=92‚Äč Final Answer 92‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: Veer has Rs. 1632 with him. He divided it amongst his sons Nitin and Pravin and asked them to invest it at 4% rate of interest compounded annually. It was seen that Nitin and Pravin got same amount after 12 and 13 years respectively. How much (in Rs. ) did Veer give to Nitin?
questions.explanation: Given: Total amount = Rs. 1632 Nitin‚Äôs investment duration = 12 years Pravin‚Äôs investment duration = 13 years Rate of interest = 4% p.a. (compounded annually) Amounts received by both are equal Formula Used: Compound Interest based Final Amount: A = P(1+R100)TP \left(1 + \frac{R}{100} \right)^TP(1+100R‚Äč)T‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let Nitin‚Äôs principal = P1, Pravin‚Äôs principal = P2 Now, ‚ÄčP1(1+4100)12=P2(1+4100)13 P1(1.04)12=P2(1.04)13 P1=1.04√óP2P1\left(1 + \frac{4}{100}\right)^{12} = P2\left(1 + \frac{4}{100}\right)^{13}\\ \ \\ P1 (1.04)^{12} = P2 (1.04)^{13} \\ \ \\P1 = 1.04 \times P2P1(1+1004‚Äč)12=P2(1+1004‚Äč)13 P1(1.04)12=P2(1.04)13 P1=1.04√óP2‚Äč‚Äč Also, P1 + P2 = 1632 Substituting P1 = 1.04 √ó P2 into the total: 1.04P2 + P2 = 1632 2.04P2 = 1632 P2 = 16322.04\frac{1632}{2.04}2.041632‚Äč‚Äč = 800 Nitin's principal = 1632 - 800 = ‚āĻ832 Exam Hall Method: ‚Äč ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/569a432c-4309-4bb0-9baf-371a0dfe0e9f.png)

questions.question: Solve the following system of linear equations: u+v+w=2 6u+4v+5w=31 5u+2v+2w=13
questions.explanation: Given Equation 1: u + v + w = 2 Equation 2: 6u + 4v + 5w = 31 Equation 3: 5u + 2v + 2w = 13 Formula Used Method of algebraic substitution for solving linear equations. Solution From Equation 1, express v + w in terms of u: v + w = 2 - u In Equation 3, factor out the 2 from the v and w terms: 5u + 2(v + w) = 13 Substitute (v + w) with (2 - u): 5u + 2(2 - u) = 13 5u + 4 - 2u = 13 3u = 9 u = 3 Now substitute u = 3 back into Equation 1: 3 + v + w = 2 v + w = -1 => w = -1 - v Substitute u = 3 and w = -1 - v into Equation 2: 6(3) + 4v + 5(-1 - v) = 31 18 + 4v - 5 - 5v = 31 13 - v = 31 -v = 18 v = -18 Finally, find w: w = -1 - (-18) = -1 + 18 = 17. The solution is u = 3, v = -18, w = 17. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: One-third of a certain wourney, is covered at a speed of 25 km/h one-fourth of the wourney is covered at a speed of 30 km/h and the rest of the wourney is covered at 50 km/h. The average speed (in km/h) of the whole wourney is:
questions.explanation: Given: One-third of wourney covered at 25km/hr One-fourth of wourney covered at 30km/hr Rest of wourney covered at 50km/hr Formula Used: Time =DistanceSpeed= \frac{Distance}{Speed}=SpeedDistance‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the total distance be x One-third of wourney =13of x=x3 \frac{1}{3} of\ x = \frac{x}{3}31‚Äčof x=3x‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Time taken to cover the distance at 25km/hr =x325=x75hr \frac{\frac{x}{3}}{25} =\frac{x}{75}hr253x‚Äč‚Äč=75x‚Äčhr‚Äč‚Äč One-fourth of wourney =14of x=x4= \frac{1}{4} of\ x = \frac{x}{4}=41‚Äčof x=4x‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Time taken to cover the distance at 30km/hr =x430=x120hr \frac{\frac{x}{4}}{30} =\frac{x}{120}hr304x‚Äč‚Äč=120x‚Äčhr‚Äč‚Äč Rest of wourney =x‚ąíx3‚ąíx4=12x‚ąí4x‚ąí3x12=5x12=x- \frac{x}{3}- \frac{x}{4} = \frac{12x -4x-3x}{12}= \frac{5x}{12}=x‚ąí3x‚Äč‚ąí4x‚Äč=1212x‚ąí4x‚ąí3x‚Äč=125x‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Time taken to cover the distance at 50km/hr = 5x1250=x120hr\frac{\frac{5x}{12}}{50} =\frac{x}{120}hr50125x‚Äč‚Äč=120x‚Äčhr‚Äč‚Äč Total time =x75+x120+x120=8x+5x+5x600=18x600=3x100= \frac{x}{75} +\frac{x}{120} + \frac{x}{120} = \frac{8x+5x+5x}{600}=\frac{18x}{600} =\frac{3x}{100}=75x‚Äč+120x‚Äč+120x‚Äč=6008x+5x+5x‚Äč=60018x‚Äč=1003x‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Average Speed =Total DistanceTotal Time=x3x100=1003=3313km/hr \frac{Total\ Distance}{Total \ Time} =\frac{x}{\frac{3x}{100}} = \frac{100}{3} =33\frac{1}{3}km/hrTotal TimeTotal Distance‚Äč=1003x‚Äčx‚Äč=3100‚Äč=3331‚Äčkm/hr‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: A batsman had a certain average for 15 innings. In the sixteenth inning, he scored 156 runs, thereby increasing his average by 8 runs. What is his new average?
questions.explanation: Given Innings before = 15 Runs in 16th inning = 156 Average increase = 8 Formula Used Total Runs = Average√óNumber of Innings\text{Average} \times \text{Number of Innings}Average√óNumber of Innings‚Äč‚Äč Solution Let the initial average be x. Total runs after 15 innings = 15x. New average after 16 innings = x + 8. Equation: 15x + 156 = 16(x + 8) 15x + 156 = 16x + 128 x = 156 - 128 = 28. New average = x + 8 = 28 + 8 = 36. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Three numbers are in the ratio 48:1116:1821\frac{4}{8} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{18}{21}84‚Äč:1611‚Äč:2118‚Äč. If the difference between the largest and the smallest numbers is 40, find the largest number.
questions.explanation: Given Ratio of three numbers = 48:1116:1821\frac{4}{8} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{18}{21}84‚Äč:1611‚Äč:2118‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Difference between the largest and the smallest numbers = 40 Solution First, simplify the given fractions: ‚Äč48=12\frac{4}{8} = \frac{1}{2}84‚Äč=21‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč 1116\frac{11}{16}1611‚Äč‚Äč remains the same. ‚Äč1821=67\frac{18}{21} = \frac{6}{7}2118‚Äč=76‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč The new ratio is 12:1116:67.\frac{1}{2} : \frac{11}{16} : \frac{6}{7}.21‚Äč:1611‚Äč:76‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč To clear the denominators, find the LCM of 2, 16, and 7, which is 112. Multiply each fraction by 112: First part = 12√ó112=56\frac{1}{2} \times 112 = 5621‚Äč√ó112=56‚Äč‚Äč Second part =1116√ó112=77 \frac{11}{16} \times 112 = 771611‚Äč√ó112=77‚Äč‚Äč Third part = 67√ó112=96\frac{6}{7} \times 112 = 9676‚Äč√ó112=96‚Äč‚Äč So, the numbers are in the ratio 56 : 77 : 96. Let the numbers be 56x, 77x, and 96x. The difference between the largest and smallest numbers is: 96x - 56x = 40 40x = 40 =&gt \Rightarrow=&gt‚Äč x = 1. The largest number is 96x = 96√ó \times √ó‚Äč1 = 96. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)
